name: Dreros 1
provenance: van Effenterre 1946; stone block from the Dreros Apollo-Delphinios temple. Eteocretan-Greek bilingual: a Greek juridical text on the same stone provides vocabulary anchors for the Eteocretan side. The most interpretively important Eteocretan text after Praisos 2.
source_citation: van Effenterre, H. (1946). 'Inscriptions archaïques crétoises.' BCH 70: 588-605. Re-edited in Duhoux 1982 §II.1; Whittaker 2017.
text: ομοσαι ομοιον τανθον δοϝαι μι ϝανται · ομαλος μο σιατας πορτα φορτου ομαι
transliteration: omosai omoion tanthon dowai mi wantai omalos mo siatas porta phortou omai
